# Copilot Instructions for vizor-echarts

- **Purpose**: Blazor wrapper around Apache ECharts; main library is src/Vizor.ECharts targeting .NET 10, with demo apps in src/Vizor.ECharts.Demo and samples in src/Vizor.ECharts.Samples.
- **Interop flow**: UI component [src/Vizor.ECharts/EChart.razor](src/Vizor.ECharts/EChart.razor) inherits from [src/Vizor.ECharts/EChartBase.cs](src/Vizor.ECharts/EChartBase.cs) and calls JS helpers in [src/Vizor.ECharts/Scripts/vizor-echarts.js](src/Vizor.ECharts/Scripts/vizor-echarts.js) (`vizorECharts.initChart/updateChart/attachClickEvent`). Chart state is serialized with custom JsonSerializerOptions and sent through JS interop.
- **Chart options model**: `ChartOptions` is a partial shell [src/Vizor.ECharts/ChartOptions.cs](src/Vizor.ECharts/ChartOptions.cs); the actual option/series types live under [src/Vizor.ECharts/Options](src/Vizor.ECharts/Options) and [src/Vizor.ECharts/Series](src/Vizor.ECharts/Series) and are mostly generated from the ECharts option.json (do not hand-edit unless deliberate; many files are large but follow consistent property naming and `[JsonPropertyName]` attributes).
- **Series typing**: Each series class implements `ISeries` and sets its `type` literal (e.g., [src/Vizor.ECharts/Series/Line/LineSeries.cs](src/Vizor.ECharts/Series/Line/LineSeries.cs)). `SeriesData<T...>` serialization is handled by [src/Vizor.ECharts/Types/SeriesDataConverterFactory.cs](src/Vizor.ECharts/Types/SeriesDataConverterFactory.cs); keep polymorphic data lists compatible with these converters.
- **External data loading**: Server-side async uses the `DataLoader` callback parameter on `EChart`; browser-side fetching uses `ExternalDataSource` and `ExternalDataSourceRef` ([src/Vizor.ECharts/Types/ExternalDataSource.cs](src/Vizor.ECharts/Types/ExternalDataSource.cs), [src/Vizor.ECharts/Types/ExternalDataSourceRef.cs](src/Vizor.ECharts/Types/ExternalDataSourceRef.cs)). Data sources serialize into fetch commands consumed by JS; `ExternalDataSource` itself cannot be serialized into options—use `ExternalDataSourceRef` inside options objects.
- **JS functions in options**: Wrap raw JavaScript snippets in `JavascriptFunction` ([src/Vizor.ECharts/Types/JavascriptFunction.cs](src/Vizor.ECharts/Types/JavascriptFunction.cs)); converter writes the raw function text into the JSON so the JS layer can `eval` it.
- **Chart grouping**: Use `ChartGroup` ([src/Vizor.ECharts/ChartGroup.cs](src/Vizor.ECharts/ChartGroup.cs)) to coordinate multiple charts and call `UpdateAsync` across all.
- **Logging/testing charts**: In DEBUG builds `vizorECharts.changeLogging(true)` is invoked to console-log serialized options and fetches; keep this in mind when diagnosing browser issues.
- **Resource cleanup**: `DisposeAsync` on `EChartBase` removes charts from groups, clears cached serializer options, and calls `vizorECharts.disposeChart` (which also drops cached external data). Call `ClearAsync` to reset chart content without disposing.
- **JS bundle/build**: JS interop lives in Scripts/vizor-echarts.js. Run `npm install` then `gulp` in src/Vizor.ECharts to build `wwwroot/js/vizor-echarts.js` and `vizor-echarts-bundle.js` (bundle includes echarts + echarts-stat). Tasks: `gulp` (default), `gulp buildJs`, `gulp buildJsBundle`, `gulp clean`.
- **.NET build**: Use `dotnet build src/Vizor.ECharts.Demo.sln` for all projects. For NuGet packages, `dotnet pack` requires Release configuration; packing in Debug fails via csproj target. The library includes README and wwwroot assets in the package.
- **Demos/samples**: Run `dotnet run --project src/Vizor.ECharts.Demo/Vizor.ECharts.Demo.csproj` to explore the Blazor demo; samples reside under src/Vizor.ECharts.Samples/Areas/*.
- **Binding generator (rarely used)**: The initial bindings were generated by src/Vizor.ECharts.BindingGenerator (see [src/Vizor.ECharts.BindingGenerator/Readme.md](src/Vizor.ECharts.BindingGenerator/Readme.md)). Only rerun when upgrading ECharts option.json; delete generated GenOptions/GenSeries folders first if regeneration fails. Steps: build echarts-docs to produce option.json, then run `option-binding --input ... --output src/Vizor.ECharts`.
- **Interop contract**: JS expects serialized options/maps/fetchOpts strings; null options trigger only loading spinner. Click events are attached via `vizorECharts.attachClickEvent` and invoke `HandleChartClick` on the component.
- **Conventions**: Prefer camelCase JSON via the shared serializer (`CacheJsonSerializerOptions=true` by default). Supply consistent custom converters through the `JsonConverters` parameter only if you understand caching implications. Avoid static `ExternalDataSource` instances; one per chart.
- **Debugging browser fetches**: External data fetch records are cached by `vizorECharts.dataSources`; references resolved via `window.vizorECharts.getDataSource(fetchId)` (see ExternalDataSourceRef converter). Paths are simple dot-separated segments evaluated in JS.

Feel free to propose additions if any key workflow or pattern is missing or unclear.
